listing revenue law of the state and
they are getting from the railroads
far more tax than the Spokesman
Review and the Turner stump speak
ers are willing to concede.


1t has just been stated that the
Northern Pacific will pay $432.32 per
mile of taxes this year and the Great
Northern will pay $44s.69 per mile Of
taxes, in each case whether that mile
be inside the limits of the city of
Spokane, where there is highly valu-
able property adjoining the railroads,
or whether 1t be in the vacant coun-
try districts, where adjoining prop
erty is not assessed at 26 cents an
acre.


According to the report of the Wis
consin tax commission Governor La
Follette's> for 1908 the average tax
paid per mile in the United States is
1894.
